scenarios.title: Vision-Language Model Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks mention OpenCLIP, open_clip_torch, CLIP/CoCa model loading, image-text embeddings, pretrained tags, tokenizers, transforms, zero-shot classification, retrieval metrics, or checkpoint conversion.
scenarios.role: Provides self-contained routing and workflow guidance for OpenCLIP model inference, evaluation, checkpoint diagnostics, and export planning.
scenarios.read_when: open_clip, open_clip_torch, create_model, create_model_and_transforms, get_tokenizer, list_pretrained, CLIP, CoCa, ViT-B-32, QuickGELU, hf-hub:, local-dir:, zero-shot, retrieval, state_dict, Hugging Face Hub export
scenarios.best_for: Loading CLIP-style models, selecting pretrained tags, creating image/text embeddings, building zero-shot classifiers, diagnosing checkpoint keys, and routing conversion/export tasks.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is general PyTorch training unrelated to OpenCLIP, generic image classification without OpenCLIP APIs, or paper-to-skills recovery rather than repository usage.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `open-clip` over generic ML/PyTorch guidance when the user names OpenCLIP/open_clip_torch APIs, model configs, pretrained tags, or OpenCLIP-specific checkpoint/evaluation behavior.

scenarios.title: OpenCLIP Training, Audio, and NaFlex Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks mention OpenCLIP training commands, open_clip_train, CSV/WebDataset data, task-era TrainingTask wrappers, FSDP, torch.compile, CLAP audio, NaFlex, GenLIP, or GenLAP.
scenarios.role: Routes complex OpenCLIP training, audio, and variable-resolution/generative workflows to focused sub-skills with safe helper scripts.
scenarios.read_when: open_clip_train.main, --dataset-type, --use-naflex, NaFlexDataConfig, CLAP-HTSAT, naflexclap, naflexgenlip, naflexgenlap, webdataset-audio, amp_bf16, FSDP, torchcompile-strategy
scenarios.best_for: Constructing training commands, validating data layouts, planning CLAP audio zero-shot, debugging NaFlex token-budget batches, and avoiding expensive or download-prone workflows by default.
scenarios.avoid_when: The user only needs generic distributed training advice with no OpenCLIP-specific APIs, or asks to reproduce a research paper from scratch rather than use this repository package.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `open-clip` when OpenCLIP-specific parser flags, dict batches, audio transforms, NaFlex patch dictionaries, or GenLIP/GenLAP semantics matter more than generic PyTorch mechanics.
